title: Bumble Q4 2023 Earnings Call Summary
text: New CEO Lidiane Jones emphasized her focus on product innovation and organizational restructuring. Total revenue increased 13% to $274 million. Bumble App revenue grew 16% to $221 million. Net loss of $32 million compared to $159 million loss in Q4 2022. Adjusted EBITDA margin of 27%. Full Year 2023: Revenue grew 16% to $1.052 billion. Added over 500,000 paying users for the second consecutive year. Q2 App Relaunch: Jones outlined a plan to reignite Bumble strength by relaunching Bumble App with a compelling modern experience geared toward capturing a broader audience. Workforce Transformation: The company announced significant restructuring with approximately $55 million in annualized cost savings, with $15 million planned for reinvestment in product engineering, AI, and safety initiatives. Executive Team Strengthening: New hires include Ali Rayl (Chief Product Officer), Antoine Leblond (Chief Technology Officer), and David Ard (Chief People Officer). 2024 Guidance: The company lowered full-year revenue growth expectations to 8-11% from prior guidance, reflecting slower-than-expected Q1 performance and Premium Plus tier underperformance.
